# Data Model & Integrity Requirements Quality Checklist: 2026 FIFA World Cup Player Data Dashboard

**Purpose**: Validate data model, entity definitions, and data integrity requirements for completeness, clarity, and consistency
**Created**: 2026-06-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Is the JSON data file schema explicitly defined with field names, types, and required/optional constraints? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK002 Are uniqueness rules defined for Player entities (what uniquely identifies a player across the dataset)? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities - Player]
- [x] CHK003 Is the relationship between League and Club entities formally specified (can a club belong to only one league)? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK004 Are the exact market value price band boundaries documented (inclusive/exclusive edge behavior)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK005 Is the "prior World Cup appearances" attribute defined — is it a count, a boolean flag, or a list of years? [Ambiguity, Spec §Key Entities - Player]
- [x] CHK006 Are the confederation-to-nation mapping rules specified (how is a nation assigned to its confederation)? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities - Confederation]
- [x] CHK007 Is the "domestic vs foreign league" classification rule defined for the national team analysis? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK008 Is "Big Five leagues" explicitly enumerated in the requirements section, or only in assumptions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK009 Are the exact age bracket boundaries specified with edge-case rules (e.g., does a 25-year-old fall into 21-25 or 26-30)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK010 Is "position" as a Player attribute scoped — which position categories are recognized (GK, DEF, MID, FWD, or finer granularity)? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities - Player]
- [x] CHK011 Is the "Other Leagues" aggregation rule defined — are all non-Big-Five leagues merged, or are some semi-prominent leagues (e.g., Eredivisie, Primeira Liga) shown separately? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Does the Player entity's "confederation" attribute conflict with the Team entity also having a "confederation" attribute (redundancy vs derived data)? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK013 Is the "OFC → Other bucket" assumption consistent with FR-004 which only lists five confederations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK014 Are the Top N ranking criteria (FR-008) consistent with available Player attributes — e.g., "tallest/shortest squads" requires height data not listed in the Player entity? [Conflict, Spec §FR-008, §Key Entities - Player]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Are data validation requirements specified for the JSON file (schema enforcement, type checking, range validation)? [Gap, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK016 Are requirements defined for handling duplicate player entries in the dataset? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK017 Is the behavior specified when a player's age falls outside all defined brackets? [Gap, Spec §FR-001, §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK018 Are requirements defined for handling a club that appears in the dataset but has no league mapping? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities - Club]
- [ ] CHK019 Is the "unavailable market value" count footnote requirement traceable to a specific FR or only in edge cases? [Traceability, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK020 Is the data compilation process documented — who compiles the JSON, what sources are authoritative, and how are conflicts resolved? [Gap, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK021 Is the "26 players per team" assumption validated against the actual 2026 World Cup squad size rules (which may change)? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK022 Are data freshness/update requirements specified — should the JSON reflect preliminary squads, final squads, or both with a version field? [Gap, Spec §Assumptions]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
